notes: Behavior evals use a disposable sandbox copied from my-app, with the IFC (aws-blocks/index.ts) replaced by the fixture specified in each eval. The executor subagent implements the prompt inside the sandbox (modifications are OK). with_skill runs instruct the agent to read skills/aws-blocks/ before implementing; without_skill runs give no skill context. The grader scores each run against the expectations array.

evals.name: persistence-uses-block-and-reads-docs
evals.prompt: I want to add an order-saving feature to my-app. Orders should have a product name, price, and quantity, stored per user, with a list endpoint. How should I implement this in aws-blocks/index.ts?
evals.expected_output: Proposes persisting orders with a Building Block (e.g. DistributedTable), not local arrays or files. Shows evidence of reading node_modules/@aws-blocks/blocks/docs/index.md then the relevant block doc. Includes per-user key design (partitionKey: userId) and a list/query endpoint.
expectations: Uses a Building Block (e.g. DistributedTable from @aws-blocks/blocks) for persistence — no local arrays, files, or in-memory stores
Transcript shows evidence of reading node_modules/@aws-blocks/blocks/docs/index.md or the relevant block doc
Orders are isolated per user with partitionKey set to a userId-equivalent field
A query or list API endpoint that returns the authenticated user's orders exists
Order records include fields for product name, price, and quantity

evals.name: sql-join-picks-distributed-database-and-mentions-dsql-limits
evals.prompt: In my TODO app I want to store TODOs and tags separately, then JOIN a user's TODOs with their tags in a list view. I'd like to use SQL — which Block should I use?
evals.expected_output: Follows the decision tree (docs/index.md) and picks a SQL Block. Default is DistributedDatabase (Aurora DSQL); Database (Aurora Serverless v2, with idle cost) only when FK/RLS/large transactions are needed. Mentions DSQL constraints (no FK, etc.) and parity limitations (OCC requires real-infra testing).
expectations: Follows the decision tree and selects a SQL Block (DistributedDatabase or Database) — does not propose merging two DistributedTables on the client side
Shows how to JOIN todos and tags using SQL
Mentions Aurora DSQL (DistributedDatabase) parity constraints (e.g. no foreign keys) or OCC/concurrency limits, and notes that real-infra testing is needed
If Database (Aurora Serverless v2) is recommended, explicitly states the concrete reasons (FK/RLS/large transactions) and the idle-cost trade-off
Shows evidence of consulting the bundled docs (node_modules/@aws-blocks/blocks/docs/index.md or the relevant block doc)

evals.name: id-rename-warns-data-loss
evals.prompt: I want to rename the 'todos' string in new DistributedTable(scope, 'todos', ...) in aws-blocks/index.ts to 'todoItems'. Please rename it.
evals.expected_output: Warns that changing the Block ID (2nd constructor argument) causes the DynamoDB table to be deleted and recreated, permanently destroying all stored data. Advises against a casual rename; treats the ID as immutable or proposes a data-migration path if truly needed. A silent rename with no warning is NG.
expectations: Clearly warns that changing the Block ID (2nd argument) triggers DynamoDB table deletion and recreation, causing permanent data loss
Does not silently rename 'todos' to 'todoItems' without warning
Recommends treating the Block ID as immutable, or — if the rename is truly necessary — proposes a data-migration procedure
Any code change shown is always accompanied by a warning; the rename is never treated as a harmless operation
